title: Mariska Hargitay to host 2026 Emmy Awards: ‘Television legend is hosting television’s biggest night’
content:
• Mariska Hargitay will host the 78th Primetime Emmy Awards on September 14, 2026, at the Peacock Theatre in LA Live, Los Angeles.
• Mariska Hargitay will be the first woman in 15 years to host the Emmy Awards.
• Hargitay is only the fourth woman to host the Emmys since 2000, following Ellen DeGeneres (2001, 2005), Heidi Klum (2008), and Jane Lynch (2011).

title: 76% of Indian consumers prefer phone calls over digital channels; spam remains a concern, shows new report
content:
• A report by Truecaller, in collaboration with Tata Tele Business Services (TTBS) and Kantar, revealed that 76% of Indian consumers prefer phone calls over digital channels for business communication.
• The Truecaller report found that 79% of businesses stated customers frequently ignore calls originating from unknown numbers, highlighting a trust issue in voice communication.
• The report noted existing regulatory frameworks such as DLT and the use of 140 and 1600 number series which aim to classify promotional and service calls.

title: Ayodhya Ram Mandir donation theft case: Champat Rai calls allegations 'baseless', to respond after SIT report
content:
• Krishna Mohan, a retired Indian Forest Service officer, has been appointed as the interim General Secretary of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust.
• The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust is expected to decide at its next meeting on 22 July whether to make Krishna Mohan's appointment as General Secretary permanent.
• The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust currently holds more than 32 kg of gold-like items and over 1,518 kg of silver and silver-like items.

title: Information & Computer services to make up 1/5th of India's proposed planned index
content:
• India's proposed monthly Index of Services Production (ISP) will have 2024-25 as its base year and will be released by the statistics ministry.
• Information and computer-related services will hold the highest weight of 21.9% in the ISP, while banking services are assigned a weight of 10.7%.
• The first trial release of the ISP, covering FY26 and April 2026, is scheduled for July 14.

title: RBI mulls guidelines on property title searches
content:
• The Supreme Court has directed the RBI and other stakeholders to collaborate on developing a standardised and practical approach for preparing title search reports before sanctioning loans, also for determining the liability of officials approving loans.
• In a January 2025 judgment, the Supreme Court, in the Central Bank of India & Another versus Smt. Prabha Jain and Others case, emphasized banks' need to be careful with inadequate title clearance reports and called for standard guidelines for fees and costs associated with them.
• The RBI is considering guidelines to standardize property title searches, as most lenders currently conduct searches for not less than 30 years, despite a clear title often being established by a shorter 13-year search.

title: Expert panel: Release services production index within 60 days
content:
• An expert panel recommended releasing the Index of Services Production (ISP) within 60 days after the reference month.
• The Statistics Ministry constituted a Technical Advisory Committee on ISP (TAC-ISP) in May 2025 under the Chairpersonship of Debjani Ghosh, Distinguished Fellow, Niti Aayog.
• The ISP is recommended to be a Laspeyres volume index with 2024-25 as the base year, and India's services sector contributes nearly 53 per cent of the country's gross value added (GVA).

title: Report proposes 'India-Plus Contribution Scorecard' to boost FDI
content:
• A report prepared by VeK Research and Pahle India Foundation proposed an 'India-Plus Contribution Scorecard' to promote FDI by rating investors on measurable economic contributions.
• The Indian economy has crossed USD 4 trillion; companies scoring above 70 on the proposed 'India-Plus Contribution Scorecard' can be given a 90-day single-window clearance.
• The report also recommended setting up a National Value Index Dashboard for enhanced policy support, while affirming that all foreign investment remains subject to national security screening.

title: Airlines seek GST on ATF at 5% with full tax credit to help cut costs
content:
• The Federation of Indian Airlines (FIA) has urged the Centre to bring Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) under the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime at a 5 per cent rate with full Input Tax Credit (ITC).
• Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) currently accounts for nearly 40 per cent of airline operating expenditure and attracts around 11 per cent Central Excise Duty along with State VAT ranging between 1 per cent and 29 per cent, none of which is available as input tax credit.
• Eliminating irrecoverable taxes on ATF is estimated to reduce delivered fuel costs by around 28 per cent, thereby lowering overall airline operating costs by nearly 8–9 per cent.

title: Missile diplomacy: Indonesia signs pact to buy India’s BrahMos and Astra missiles
content:
• Indonesia signed initial agreements to purchase India’s BrahMos supersonic missiles and Astra beyond-visual-range air-to-air missiles during Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Jakarta on Tuesday.
• For the BrahMos missile, BrahMos Aerospace (a joint venture between India’s Defence Research and Development Organisation - DRDO - and Russia’s NPO Mashinostroyeniya) signed the contract with Indonesia’s Defence Ministry.
• Indonesia’s private defence company Republikorp and India’s state-owned Bharat Dynamics Ltd signed the agreement for the Astra missiles; Indonesia is the first country to acquire Astra and the third potential foreign buyer of BrahMos after Vietnam and the Philippines.

title: Why has India cut broken grain share in PDS rice under Rice Milling Transformation scheme?
content:
• The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) approved the Rice Milling Transformation (RMT) scheme, reducing the broken grain share in PDS raw rice from 25% to 10% and in parboiled rice from 16% to 5%.
• As of June 1, India's Central Pool held a record high of 122.64 million tonnes (mt) of foodgrain stock, including 42.84 mt of paddy, which the new scheme aims to process.
• Under the Open Market Sale Scheme (OMSS) policy, the 15% separated broken rice will be auctioned from rice millers’ premises at a base price of ₹20/kg, which is lower than the ₹23.20/kg price announced for ethanol distilleries.
